body.from: Comments now trigger, and should log this change in comment body.

comment.body:
refactor(audit): simplify LAST_100 columns and use comment-specific links
    
    Streamline LAST_100 readability and link precision.
    
    - remove Details column from LAST_100 table
    - render Issue column as linked issue number only
    - render Milestone column as linked milestone number only
    - prefer comment.html_url for target links (issuecomment anchors)
      with fallback to issue/milestone URLs
    
    Detailed before/after content remains available via per-event artifact links in the Event ID column.

body.from: Hash organized diffs should now be available.

comment.body:
fix(audit): prefix every edited comment line in diff block
    
    Update comment body diff rendering to apply +/- prefixes to each line for multiline edits.
    
    - replace single printf lines with line-wise sed prefixing
    - preserve fenced diff block format for GitHub red/green rendering
    
    This ensures multiline comment edits are fully represented in per-event markdown diffs.

body.from:
refactor(audit): simplify LAST_100 columns and use comment-specific links
    
    Streamline LAST_100 readability and link precision.
    
    - remove Details column from LAST_100 table
    - render Issue column as linked issue number only
    - render Milestone column as linked milestone number only
    - prefer comment.html_url for target links (issuecomment anchors)
      with fallback to issue/milestone URLs
    
    Detailed before/after content remains available via per-event artifact links in the Event ID column.

milestone.title: NonPriority
milestone.description: Just a placeholder for issues that should be fixed, but not currently within an existing milestone.

milestone.title: PermissionHandlingUpdatesTEST
milestone.description:
# Milestone Summary
Initializing  publishers blocks the main thread  leading to an unresponsive application.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Objective

Use **Kotlin Coroutines** to eliminate main thread blocking and enable
smooth publisher initialization.

# Publisher Initialization Blocking UI

## Current State

After the device connects, the app calls:

`onSerialReady()`

Inside this method:

`publisherManager.initializePublishers()`
`publisherManager.startPublishers()`

During initialization, the app:

-   Requests several permissions
-   Waits for them to be granted

This process blocks the **UI completely**, causing the app to become
unresponsive.

## Expected State

In `AbcvlibActivity`:

-   Move the `onSerialReady` callback execution context to the **Default
    Dispatcher**

This will:

-   Allow publishers to initialize asynchronously
-   Handle permission requests without blocking the UI

TEST

# Milestone Summary
Initializing  publishers blocks the main thread  leading to an unresponsive application.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Objective

Use **Kotlin Coroutines** to eliminate main thread blocking and enable
smooth publisher initialization.

# Publisher Initialization Blocking UI

## Current State

After the device connects, the app calls:

`onSerialReady()`

Inside this method:

`publisherManager.initializePublishers()`
`publisherManager.startPublishers()`

During initialization, the app:

-   Requests several permissions
-   Waits for them to be granted

This process blocks the **UI completely**, causing the app to become
unresponsive.

## Expected State

In `AbcvlibActivity`:

-   Move the `onSerialReady` callback execution context to the **Default
    Dispatcher**

This will:

-   Allow publishers to initialize asynchronously
-   Handle permission requests without blocking the UI

In the absence of the custom robot hardware, it would be useful to have an instrumentation test that mimics the communication between the two. In this way both remote and automated tests could be performed at runtime.

https://github.com/tekkura/smartphone-robot-android/blob/3f31a3b4769493a56f6d145aeb8aaa41c6ca0cdb/libs/abcvlib/src/main/java/jp/oist/abcvlib/util/SerialCommManager.java#L110

That is the function that would have to be called by the test, with command = SET_MOTOR_LEVELS as thats really the only one being used right now. You'd have to create a test definition for an instance of https://github.com/tekkura/smartphone-robot-android/blob/main/libs/abcvlib/src/main/java/jp/oist/abcvlib/util/RP2040State.java.

## USB-Serial scope
Issues under this milestone are also related to the USB-serial communication protocol.

The main focus is:
1. Refactor low-level handling of start/end flags and other enveloping in favor of robust high-level read/write abstractions with intuitive defaults for common USB-serial errors/exceptions.
2. Reduce communication loop time between phone and firmware. Current round-trip latency is approximately 20 ms; identify contributors via targeted profiling and optimize accordingly.

### Background
Another scope is currently focused on refactoring the firmware of the external USB connected robot to use USB-CDC via TinyUSB instead of the current stdio-based USB-serial implementation. Many issues encountered during this project appear to stem from instability in the low-level USB-serial layer. By migrating to TinyUSB on RP2040, firmware aims to improve stability, separation of concerns, and long-term maintainability.

This work is dependent on progress in the firmware repo, so implementation that depends on firmware protocol changes should wait until firmware-side readiness is established.

TODO:
Add details about the expected communication to mimic.

comment.body: No, this is not the way to bring the changes from fork

issue.title: Evaluate Python Android framework candidates with explicit selection criteria

comment.body:
> I don't think of it as a good idea. Now with the advent of claude code or openai codex, developing on platforms/languages of which a user doesn't have much knowledge, has become easy. The research users potentially would be fast to develop natively via claude code than to learn the python framework for android.

A fair argument 😅 but we have this scope approved and funded, so we need to finish these points at least. Implementing it in great depth is outside the scope, just an evaluation on existing options. Most researchers go through mandatory coding courses at OIST and python is the most used language for that, thus the motivation to provide something with similar syntax. I still think its a good idea though as even if you depend on LLMs, its still good to know how to at least read your own code.

issue.title: Evaluate Python Android framework candidates with explicit selection criteria

comment.body: Also another argument is that these researchers need to develop their own novel algorithms for their research. These are things LLMs don't know anything about, so they can't help. They can of course depend on them for syntax fixing and explaining code, etc, but for generating their research ideas it wouldn't work as the research isn't published anywhere for an LLM to know about.

with the guide contents. 

Also another thing I'd like to confirm here. The dependency on the `io.github.nishkarsh:android-permissions` was to be refactored out in favor of modern native methods to Android. 

It is still in:

  - Dependency wired in module:
      - libs/abcvlib/build.gradle.kts:48 → api(libs.android.permissions)
  - Version catalog alias:
      - gradle/libs.versions.toml:77 → io.github.nishkarsh:android-permissions
  - Actual API usage in code:
      - libs/abcvlib/src/main/java/jp/oist/abcvlib/core/inputs/Publisher.java:11
      - libs/abcvlib/src/main/java/jp/oist/abcvlib/core/inputs/Publisher.java:54
      - libs/abcvlib/src/main/java/jp/oist/abcvlib/core/inputs/phone/MicrophoneData.java:14

Does this fit easily into this milestone for you? This was one of the original main goals of it.

comment.body:
@topherbuckley 
- The original plan to refactor permissions handling was mainly to fix the UI freezing caused by the Java-based implementation. 
- Now initializing publishers inside a coroutine makes the freezing issue is resolved.

- The library ` io.github.nishkarsh:android-permissions` still functioning well. We can either 
**keep it as is**, or 
**refactor it to use modern native Android permission APIs**

But that would extend the scope of this milestone.

issue.title: AbcvlibController– Some Properties Violate the Java2Kotlin Migration Guide
AbcvlibController– Some Properties Violate the Java2Kotlin Migration Guide

body.from:
### Summary

### Summary

Both `_isRunning` and `_output` in [`AbcvlibController`](https://github.com/tekkura/sr-android/pull/163/changes#diff-3ca45e1bf592ec5582592836a84968c58fc6e67fc92139baba37e6b48fd21d16) do not follow the [Java2Kotlin Migration Guide](https://github.com/tekkura/sr-android/blob/milestone/java2kotlin/docs/migrations/Java2Kotlin.md).

> Do not bypass JVM accessor clashes by renaming canonical properties to underscore-backed names (for example, `state` → `_state`) when the rename is only used to avoid getter/setter conflicts.

### Current state

```kotlin
private var _isRunning = false
private var _output: Output = Output()
```

### Expected state

```kotlin
private var isRunning = false
private var output: Output = Output()
```

@Ma7moud3ly Done. I rebased both milestone/PermissionHandlingUpdates onto main then PermissionHandlingUpdates/Migration-Guide onto milestone/PermissionHandlingUpdates

@topherbuckley thanks
waiting for your comments or approval
@Ma7moud3ly I have a few questions on Issue #14 before I can review this with a good enough understanding. See my comment here: https://github.com/tekkura/sr-android/issues/14#issuecomment-3980117890

@topherbuckley 
> Rather than just wrapping the `onSerialReady` contents inside a `lifecycleScope.launch` call? i.e.

### This will not work as expected ❌
- We would need to apply this change across all 9 apps
- `onSerialReady` in `AbcvlibActivity` is already overridden and is invoked from `MainActivity`.

For example, in `BasicQrreceiver`:

```kotlin
override fun onSerialReady(usbSerial: UsbSerial) {
    publisherManager = PublisherManager()

    val qrCodeData = QRCodeData.Builder(this, publisherManager, this).build()
    qrCodeData.addSubscriber(this)

    publisherManager.initializePublishers()
    publisherManager.startPublishers()

    setSerialCommManager(SerialCommManager(usbSerial))
    super.onSerialReady(usbSerial)
}
```
-  All of the `MainActivity.onSerialReady` logic would remain at the main thread.
- Only `super.onSerialReady(usbSerial)` content and `onOutputsReady` will be shifted
- The apps will still unresponsive

@topherbuckley 
> What happened to the apparent need to "Move permission handling to the Activity." and "Request permissions before initializing the USB connection."? That you outlined in the issue description? I'm not seeing this mentioned in the kotlin version of the fix, or the migration guide you proposed in https://github.com/tekkura/sr-android/pull/169/changes. Can you clarify if this is no longer needed or should be also included in the migration guide?

- I have explained before that we don't need to modify anything related to the permissions handling.. Kotlin coroutines can make everything work as expected with minor changes.   https://github.com/tekkura/sr-android/issues/14#issuecomment-3966306015
- While the millstone refers to update permissions handling, the actual changes are related to fix the main thread blocking made by requesting permissions at `Publisher.kt`

@topherbuckley 
> Rather than just wrapping the `onSerialReady` contents inside a `lifecycleScope.launch` call? i.e.

### This will not work as expected ❌
- We would need to apply this change across all 9 apps
- `onSerialReady` in `AbcvlibActivity` is already overridden and is invoked from `MainActivity`.

For example, in `BasicQrreceiver`:

```kotlin
override fun onSerialReady(usbSerial: UsbSerial) {
    publisherManager = PublisherManager()

    val qrCodeData = QRCodeData.Builder(this, publisherManager, this).build()
    qrCodeData.addSubscriber(this)

    publisherManager.initializePublishers()
    publisherManager.startPublishers()

    setSerialCommManager(SerialCommManager(usbSerial))
    super.onSerialReady(usbSerial)
}
```
-  All of the `MainActivity.onSerialReady` logic would remain at the main thread.
- Only `super.onSerialReady(usbSerial)` content and `onOutputsReady` will be shifted
- The apps will still unresponsive 
- The proposed solution aims to centralize one fix to all apps
- It will not use lambada
The expected changed:
 ```kotlin
            this.usbSerial = UsbSerial(
                context = this,
                usbManager = usbManager,
                serialReadyListener = object : SerialReadyListener {
                    override fun onSerialReady(usbSerial: UsbSerial) {
                        lifecycleScope.launch(Dispatchers.Default) {
                            this@AbcvlibActivity.onSerialReady(usbSerial)
                        }
                    }
                }
            )
```

body.from:
@topherbuckley 
> Rather than just wrapping the `onSerialReady` contents inside a `lifecycleScope.launch` call? i.e.

### This will not work as expected ❌
- We would need to apply this change across all 9 apps
- `onSerialReady` in `AbcvlibActivity` is already overridden and is invoked from `MainActivity`.

For example, in `BasicQrreceiver`:

```kotlin
override fun onSerialReady(usbSerial: UsbSerial) {
    publisherManager = PublisherManager()

    val qrCodeData = QRCodeData.Builder(this, publisherManager, this).build()
    qrCodeData.addSubscriber(this)

    publisherManager.initializePublishers()
    publisherManager.startPublishers()

    setSerialCommManager(SerialCommManager(usbSerial))
    super.onSerialReady(usbSerial)
}
```
-  All of the `MainActivity.onSerialReady` logic would remain at the main thread.
- Only `super.onSerialReady(usbSerial)` content and `onOutputsReady` will be shifted
- The apps will still unresponsive

comment.body: @topherbuckley I don't think using gradle.properties is a good idea since this file is version-controlled and thus publicly available.

> ### This will not work as expected ❌
> 
>     * We would need to apply this change across all 9 apps
> 
>     * `onSerialReady` in `AbcvlibActivity` is already overridden and is invoked from `MainActivity`.

Understood. That makes sense. 

> * I have explained before that we don't need to modify anything related to the permissions handling.. Kotlin coroutines can make everything work as expected with minor changes.   [PermissionHandlingUpdates: Permissions Handling ANR Issue #14 (comment)](https://github.com/tekkura/sr-android/issues/14#issuecomment-3966306015)

This didn't answer my question. 
Your proposed kotlin version of the fix in https://github.com/tekkura/sr-android/issues/14#issuecomment-3966306015
Does what "Suggested fix 1" from https://github.com/tekkura/sr-android/issues/14#issue-3810091744 does (i.e. it allocates the calls to another thread). What I am asking is why the "Suggested fix 2" is no longer a part of the kotlin solution, and if it is not necessary, then why not. I'm not arguing for it to stay, I just want to understand why it became unnecessary. 

> [@topherbuckley](https://github.com/topherbuckley) I know turning from one solution to another in the same issue is confusing
> So we can close this and move to [#170](https://github.com/tekkura/sr-android/issues/170)

I'd prefer we keep everything related to this in a single issue if possible. I don't see the benefit of starting a new issue that duplicates content from here.

comment.body:
> @topherbuckley I don't think using gradle.properties is a good idea since this file is version-controlled and thus publicly available.

Agreed! Looks good to me.
#codex-review

@GeorgeChernyshov @topherbuckley

As a part of phase 1, I reviewed the `ObjectDetector` Subscriber to understand the expected changes. I noticed that the class exposes `onObjectsDetected` callback to three apps: `handsOnApp`, `basicSubscriber`, and `basicCharger`.

```kotlin
override fun onObjectsDetected(
    bitmap: Bitmap,
    tensorImage: TensorImage,
    results: MutableList<Detection>,
    inferenceTime: Long,
    height: Int,
    width: Int
)
```

Tensorflow dependencies 

- `tensorImage` is currently not used anywhere.
- `List<Detection>` or the new mediapipe `List<ObjectDetectorResult>` could be mapped to a **custom data class**. This would fully decouple the apps from the internal object detector implementation.
```
CustomDetection #0:
  Box: (x: , y: , w: , h: )
  Categories:
    index      :
    score      :
    class name :
```

```kotlin
override fun onObjectsDetected(
    bitmap: Bitmap,
    results: List<CustomDetection>,
    inferenceTime: Long,
    height: Int,
    width: Int
)
```

Benefits:
- Clear separation of concerns between the detector and apps.
- Easier to maintain, extend, and refactor.
- The library user does not need to implement TensorFlow or MediaPipe directly.
Rebased the branch onto main
Rebased both branch and PR onto `milestone/AppSigning`
